what_it_does: It is a generated surface specification for view E2 in area E, describing a desktop-only UI surface titled 'Embedding chain embed→similarity→retrieve' that belongs to journey J2 and maps to three UI addresses (canvas node join, inspector model-field default-brain, workshop ports), while declaring build targets and design constraints.
observations: A generated surface-spec for view E2 under company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J2/E2.json
Represents a desktop-only surface for the embedding chain: embed→similarity→retrieve
Home journey is J2 and journeys list contains only J2; no cross_journeys
Addresses listed are ui://canvas/node/join, ui://inspector/model-field/default-brain, and ui://workshop/ports
Build targets include canvas, inspector, and workshop components
Design lint command is check.py --target canvas/app/src --fail-on
Status is planned; represents MOD-embed and NODE-ports
standouts: The title encodes a data-flow chain (embed→similarity→retrieve)
Three distinct UI addresses span canvas, inspector, and workshop
Explicit build_to constraints: design lint and a single token source
conventions: Uses a structured JSON schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to
References UI addresses with ui:// scheme
Declares build_to components, lint command, and token pipeline
concerns: The _what field claims addresses must never be invented, but this file itself is generated and we cannot verify address existence from this file alone
cross_journeys is empty despite the surface spanning three components, which may or may not be intentional
Status is planned so implementation details may still change
notes: This is a planned desktop surface spec for the embedding retrieval flow in journey J2, tying three UI addresses to canvas, inspector, and workshop build targets.
questions: Are the three addresses (canvas node join, inspector default-brain, workshop ports) all part of the same view state or separate entry points?
What does 'default-brain' refer to in the inspector model-field address?
How does the embedding chain interact with MOD-embed and NODE-ports modules in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view E2 carrying the web of address-refs for the embedding chain; the file does exactly that, listing addresses, journeys, and build targets consistently.
apparent_themes: Strict separation of generated specs from implementation
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a canonical, buildable UI surface that connects an embedding/similarity/retrieval workflow to concrete canvas, inspector, and workshop components, while enforcing a single token source and a lint gate.
novelty: The surface title models a pipeline (embed→similarity→retrieve) and maps it to a small set of cross-component UI addresses, making the data-flow stage visible in the spec.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, address-level surface definition for the embedding chain within journey J2, including explicit build_to targets and design constraints that a fused system can use to locate and validate the relevant UI parts.
summary_for_embedding: A planned desktop surface specification (E2) for journey J2 that maps the embedding chain embed→similarity→retrieve to three UI addresses across canvas, inspector, and workshop components and declares build and token constraints.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a buildable, lint-gated surface definition linking the embedding/similarity/retrieval workflow to specific UI addresses and components.
suggested_fields: address_semantics: what each ui:// address means or controls
view_state_binding: how the three addresses compose a single view E2
platform_exclusion_reason: why only desktop is supported
